channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: 2 San Jose men arrested after joyriding in stolen vehicle: police
item.description: (KRON) -- Two San Jose residents were arrested after possessing a stolen vehicle, according to the Benicia Police Department.
item.dc:creator: Jordan Baker, NewsNation

item.title: Bears fire coach Matt Eberflus after skid marked by poor decisions late in games
item.description: CHICAGO (AP) — Matt Eberflus was fired by the Chicago Bears on Friday, one day after botching a timeout in a loss at Detroit. Offensive coordinator Thomas Brown will serve as interim coach, the Bears said in a statement.
item.dc:creator: ANDREW SELIGMAN and ROB MAADDI, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Former Tennessee head coach Bill Battle dies at 82
item.description: KNOXVILLE, Tenn. (WATE) -- Former University of Tennessee Coach William "Bill" Battle III died on Thanksgiving morning at the age of 82, according to the university.
item.dc:creator: Hope McAlee, NewsNation

item.title: Lake-effect snow heading for New York state could disrupt travel and football
item.description: BUFFALO, N.Y. (AP) — Black Friday was giving way to a white weekend in parts of New York state, with the first big snow of the season threatening to bury towns along lakes Erie and Ontario during a hectic holiday travel and shopping weekend.
item.dc:creator: CAROLYN THOMPSON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Amazon worker strike: 5 things to know
item.description: The strike, dubbed "Make Amazon Pay," has planned demonstrations in more than 20 countries including the United States, and the protests are slated to last through Cyber Monday.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, WGN 9

item.title: Auto industry may lose 17 percent of profits due to Trump’s tariff plan: Report
item.description: Carmakers in the U.S. and Europe could lose up to 17 percent of their combined annual core profits in a worst-case scenario if the U.S. imposes steep tariffs on Europe, and two key U.S. allies, Mexico and Canada, a report from S&P Global released Friday showed.
item.dc:creator: Juliann Ventura, ABC 4

item.title: Arkansas police investigating Black Friday shooting at mall in Little Rock
item.description: Little Rock, Arkansas, police were investigating an "isolated" shooting on Black Friday at the Park Plaza Mall in the afternoon.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: British lawmakers move closer to allowing terminally ill adults to end their lives
item.description: Members of Parliament approved the assisted dying bill by a 330-275 vote, signaling their approval in principle for the bill, which will undergo further scrutiny before it goes to a final vote.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Watch: Colorado officer uses kid's bike during pursuit
item.description: A Lone Tree police officer went above and beyond to try and catch a theft suspect who fled on a scooter in a neighborhood.
item.dc:creator: Maddie Rhodes, KRON4

item.title: Alabama A&M player Medrick Burnett Jr. dies following injury during game
item.description: BIRMINGHAM, Ala. (WIAT) — Medrick Burnett Jr., a 20-year-old football player for Alabama A&M University, died Wednesday a month after sustaining a serious injury during a football game. Burnett Jr., a linebacker who was injured during the Magic City Classic on Oct.
item.dc:creator: Drew Taylor, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Cucumbers sold in 26 states recalled over Salmonella concerns
item.description: A produce company based in Arizona is recalling whole cucumbers that were distributed in more than two dozen states and Canada due to Salmonella concerns.
item.dc:creator: Kyler Swaim, Fox 5

item.title: Cucumbers sold in 26 states recalled over Salmonella concerns
item.description: A produce company based in Arizona is recalling whole cucumbers that were distributed in more than two dozen states and Canada due to Salmonella concerns.
item.dc:creator: Kyler Swaim, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: SunFed cucumbers and Costco eggs recalled due to potential salmonella contamination
item.description: Cucumbers shipped to the U.S. and Canada — and organic eggs sold in 25 Costco stores in five southern U.S. states — have been recalled because of potential salmonella contamination.
item.dc:creator: Kenya Hunter, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: From T-shirts to thongs, how indie film merchandise became a hot commodity
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — On a recent Saturday afternoon, hundreds of “Anora” fans lined up for hours on Los Angeles' Melrose Avenue, hoping to snag exclusive merchandise inspired by Sean Baker’s latest film about a stripper who marries the son of a Russian oligarch.
item.dc:creator: KRYSTA FAURIA, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Woman to reunite with brother missing for 25 years after spotting his picture in news
item.description: (KTLA) - A man missing since 1999 has been identified and is set to reunite with his family after media coverage helped bring the decades-long case to a close.
item.dc:creator: Travis Schlepp, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Retailers coax Black Friday shoppers into stores with big discounts and giveaways
item.description: Retailers in the U.S. have used giveaways and bigger-than expected discounts to reward shoppers who ventured out on Black Friday.
item.dc:creator: Anne D'Innocenzio, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Suspected gunman who fired more than 200 rounds at Florida condo units killed by sniper's bullet, sheriff says
item.description: Florida authorities shot and killed a man Thursday who allegedly fired more than 200 rounds at neighboring units in a condominium complex.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Arizona executions to resume, breaking 2-year pause during review of state death penalty procedures
item.description: PHOENIX (AP) — Executions will resume in Arizona following a two-year pause, the state's top prosecutor says.
item.dc:creator: ANITA SNOW, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Northeast hit with rain, snow amid Thanksgiving Day festivities
item.description: The U.S. Northeast has been hit with a winter storm that has brought rain and snow amid Thanksgiving Day festivities and disrupted travel plans for those moving around on the holiday.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, WGN 9

item.title: Is your turkey dry? This might be why
item.description: Every year in the weeks leading up to Thanksgiving, the holiday conversation inevitably turns to one question regarding the annual feast: is turkey any good? While debates and discussions about which side dishes are the best, it seems like the star of the show often gets overlooked, or worse, malign
item.dc:creator: Travis Schlepp, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Why retailers still see Black Friday as the high point of the holiday shopping season
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — After weeks of plugging increasingly inviting discounts, retailers in the United States and several other countries are preparing for prime time: Black Friday, the bargain bonanza that still reigns as the unofficial kickoff of the holiday shopping season even if it's lost some luster
item.dc:creator: ANNE D'INNOCENZIO, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: US faith-based missionaries build homes for Mexican poor south of the border
item.description: A group of 150 faith-based volunteers from North Texas traveled south of the border in the days before Thanksgiving and built several homes for the poor in Reynosa, Mexico. One family sat down with Border Report and detailed their experience.
item.dc:creator: Sandra Sanchez, Fox 5

item.title: A woman was called into work on her day off. She left a lottery winner
item.description: Nobody likes being called into work on their day off. But for one California woman, a last-minute call-in would ultimately change her life for the better.
item.dc:creator: Travis Schlepp, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump nominees targeted in series of threats, spokesperson says
item.description: Multiple nominees and appointees tapped to serve in President-elect Trump’s incoming administration were targeted with threats this week, a spokesperson for the transition said Wednesday, though it was unclear how many were affected.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Fired workers blamed for knocking over 120 headstones at Tennessee cemetery
item.description: More than 120 headstones at a Tennessee cemetery were knocked over by freshly fired employees, according to the cemetery's owner.
item.dc:creator: Shay Arthur, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Fugitive on FBI's 'Most Wanted Terrorist' list captured after more than 20 years on run
item.description: Daniel Andreas San Diego, a fugitive on the FBI’s “Most Wanted Terrorist” list, was captured in the United Kingdom on Monday after more than 20 years.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, KRON4

item.title: San Francisco watchdog Ricci Wynne arrested, suspected of pimping
item.description: Wynne has legions of social media followers. Over the past three years, videos posted on his social media pages, RawRicci415, garnered hundreds of thousands of views.
item.dc:creator: Amy Larson, WGN 9

item.title: Trump's threat to impose tariffs could raise prices for consumers, colliding with promise for relief
item.description: DETROIT (AP) — If Donald Trump makes good on his threat to slap 25% tariffs on everything imported from Mexico and Canada, the price increases that could follow will collide with his campaign promise to give American families a break from inflation.
item.dc:creator: TOM KRISHER and CHRISTOPHER RUGABER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Tank explodes, catches fire near GM facility in Michigan
item.description: A tank containing byproducts from oil drilling operations exploded and caught fire Tuesday near the General Motors Proving Ground in southeastern Michigan, damaging two nearby homes but causing no injuries, a fire chief said.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Israel, Lebanon's Hezbollah agree to a ceasefire to end nearly 14 months of fighting
item.description: Israel approved a United States-brokered ceasefire agreement with Lebanon's Hezbollah on Tuesday, setting the stage for an end to nearly 14 months of fighting linked to the ongoing war in the Gaza Strip.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Virginia pilot, dog killed in New York plane crash; 2 other dogs found alive
item.description: A Virginia pilot and a rescue dog he was transporting were killed after a plane he was flying crashed in New York on Sunday, authorities said.
item.dc:creator: Jenny Gable, Fox 5

item.title: WATCH: Man throws coffee at charging elk in Colorado
item.description: A video has surfaced of a man throwing what appears to be coffee at a bull elk at Evergreen Lake in Colorado.
item.dc:creator: Greg Nieto, Fox 5

item.title: Biden proposes Medicare, Medicaid cover costly weight-loss drugs like like Wegovy or Ozempic
item.description: A bipartisan coalition of congressional members have lobbied for the drugs to be covered by Medicare, saying it could save the government from spending billions of dollars on treating chronic ailments that stem from obesity.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Couple charged in ring suspected of stealing $1 million in Lululemon clothes
item.description: A Connecticut couple has been charged in Minnesota with being part of a shoplifting ring suspected of stealing around $1 million in goods across the country.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump's favorability climbs in post-election poll
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump’s favorability climbed by six points after he was elected president earlier this month, a new poll on Tuesday found.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, Fox 5

item.title: Walmart becomes largest company to roll back diversity, equity and inclusion policies
item.description: Walmart, the world’s largest retailer, is rolling back its diversity, equity and inclusion policies, joining a growing list of major corporations that have done the same after coming under attack by conservative activists.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Biden proposes Medicare and Medicaid cover costly weight-loss drugs for millions of obese Americans
item.description: Millions of Americans with obesity would be eligible to have popular weight-loss drugs like Wegovy or Ozempic covered by Medicare or Medicaid under a new rule the Biden administration proposed Tuesday morning.
item.dc:creator: AMANDA SEITZ Associated Press, WFLA, NBC
